messages.content:
You just completed a Slay the Spire 2 run as the silent.
Result: DEFEAT at Floor 27 (fitness: 91.7)
Combats won: 6/6
Run duration: 3886.8s

## Full-run Decision Digest

## Strategic Thread (agent intent at decision time — hypotheses, not ground truth)
### F14
- [card_reward] Foundation plan: Setup scaling and powers during Lagavulin's sleep phase, then burst damage. Look for draw and more Shiv generation to fully utilize Phantom Blades.
### F15
- [card_reward] Committed to Shiv/Discard hybrid engine: use targeted discard like Hidden Daggers to trigger Sly cards (Abrasive) for free scaling, then use Phantom Blades to turn generated Shivs into massive burst. Need more Sly payoffs and card draw to ensure consistency.
### F16
- [rest_site] Hybrid Shiv/Discard foundation: wins by scaling with Phantom Blades and Abrasive (played via Sly), retaining Shivs. Needs more draw/block and reliable discard; avoid generic attacks.
### F17
- [card_reward] Foundation plan: survive with Phantom Blades and efficient block while looking for a definitive scaling engine. Malaise handles multi-hit bosses, so prioritize finding consistent weak/vulnerable application, more cycle, or a dedicated win condition (like Catalyst or Envenom). Skip narrow synergy cards until the engine is secured.
### F18
- [event] Foundation Nightmare/Flask plan: use Suppress+ for early mitigation, then setup Nightmare on Bouncing Flask or defensive tools to win. Needs more consistent block and card draw.
- [map] Foundation deck: currently a basic collection of strikes and defends with minimal scaling. Priority is finding a core engine—either poison or shivs—while using shops for card removal and rest sites for essential upgrades to survive the high damage of Act 2 hallways.
### F19
- [card_reward] Committed poison/control plan: Nightmare the Bouncing Flask or Malaise for boss fights, use Suppress+ and shivs to survive hallways. Need more consistent draw, block, and poison multipliers (like Accelerant); avoid adding off-plan attacks.
### F20
- [event] Building around Malaise and Nightmare+; need to find reliable block or damage mitigation for Act 2 elites.
### F21
- [card_reward] Foundation plan: Survive with frontload damage and efficient control (Suppress+, Malaise) while using WLP and Nightmare to set up win conditions (e.g. duplicating Bouncing Flask or Malaise). Keep an eye out for card draw and a reliable scaling engine.
### F22
- [hand_select] Retaining Nightmare+ for potential use if the fight doesn't end next turn, or simply because Strike has no value to retain.
- [card_reward] Foundation plan: use Phantom Blades and Shiv generators for efficient damage while using Malaise and Suppress+ for control. Looking for Accuracy or poison scaling (Envenom/Catalyst) as a primary boss engine.
- [map] Foundation deck: currently a basic collection of strikes and defends with minimal scaling. Priority is finding a core engine—either poison or shivs—while using shops for card removal and rest sites for essential upgrades to survive the high damage of Act 2 hallways.
### F23
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: use Blade Dance, Cloak and Dagger, and Phantom Blades to generate shivs buffed by Accuracy. Prioritize exhaust/discard outlets and card draw. Skip basic attacks.
- [map] Foundation deck focusing on basic survival; urgently needs a scaling engine like poison or shivs and more card removal. Avoid combats in Act 2 to preserve HP while hunting for key powers at events and rest sites.
### F24
- [event] Committed Shiv plan: utilize Accuracy, Phantom Blades, and Blade Dance for burst damage. Retain key cards with Well-Laid Plans and use Nightmare on high-value targets like Accuracy or Malaise. Need more block and card draw.
### F25
- [rest_site] Committed Shiv engine: use Accuracy, Phantom Blades, and Well-Laid Plans to set up massive burst damage turns with Shiv generators. Defend while scaling, then unleash retained Shivs. Needs more card draw to consistently find powers and avoid bricking.
### F26
- [map] Foundation deck: currently a basic collection of strikes and defends with minimal scaling. Priority is finding a core engine—either poison or shivs—while using shops for card removal and rest sites for essential upgrades to survive the high damage of Act 2 hallways.
### F27
- [event] Committed Shiv plan: utilize Accuracy, Phantom Blades, and Blade Dance for burst damage. Retain key cards with Well-Laid Plans and use Nightmare on high-value targets like Accuracy or Malaise. Need more block and card draw.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: scale with Accuracy and Phantom Blades, generate shivs with Blade Dance and Exterminate, use Nightmare on scaling or big block. Need better block scaling and draw.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: rely on Blade Dance and Cloak and Dagger to generate shivs, scaled by Accuracy and Phantom Blades. Prioritize card draw (Acrobatics, Backflip) and defensive scaling (Footwork) to survive burst damage while outputting shivs.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: generate shivs with Blade Dance/Cloak and Dagger, scale with Accuracy and Phantom Blades. Defend with Cloak and Dagger and basic block while looking for better defensive scaling and draw.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: play Phantom Blades and Accuracy, generate Shivs to deal massive damage, and use Nightmare on key scaling or block cards. Needs defensive scaling and draw; avoid generic attacks.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: play Phantom Blades and Accuracy, then spam shivs with Blade Dance, Cloak and Dagger, and Leading Strike; use Nightmare on strong powers or block. We still need more draw to find our powers faster. Avoid adding non-shiv attacks.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: play powers (Accuracy, Phantom Blades), generate shivs (Blade Dance, Cloak and Dagger, Leading Strike), and use Pocketwatch or Well-Laid Plans for consistency. Needs more draw and block scaling. Skip generic attacks.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: scale with Accuracy+ and Phantom Blades, then generate shivs. Need reliable block and draw engines to sustain through Act 2 and 3. Avoid adding generic damage.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: rely on Blade Dance and Cloak and Dagger for shiv generation scaled by Accuracy and Phantom Blades. Retain Nightmare for key powers or strong mitigation. Still need consistent draw and a reliable block engine to survive burst damage; skip basic attacks.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: scale with Accuracy and Phantom Blades, generate shivs with Blade Dance and Cloak and Dagger. Need more block and card draw. Remove remaining Strikes when possible.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: generate shivs with Blade Dance/Cloak and Dagger, scale with Accuracy/Phantom Blades, and utilize draw to keep the engine going. Prioritize removing remaining Strikes.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: use Blade Dance, Cloak and Dagger, and Phantom Blades with Accuracy+ for damage. Prioritize draw and retain key pieces. Needs defensive scaling like Footwork or Afterimage; skip generic attacks.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: generate shivs with Blade Dance and Cloak and Dagger, scaled by Accuracy+ and Phantom Blades. Use Nightmare+ on key scaling or defensive pieces. Needs more draw and defensive scaling (Footwork/Afterimage) to survive Act 2/3.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: generate shivs with Blade Dance/Cloak and Dagger, scale damage with Accuracy/Phantom Blades, and rely on Nightmare for huge burst. Keep deck thin of basic cards to quickly cycle to shiv generators and defense.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: generate shivs to scale with Accuracy+ and Phantom Blades, while using Well-Laid Plans to set up Nightmare+ on key powers. We need more card draw (Acrobatics, Backflip) to cycle efficiently and find our engine fast.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: scale with Accuracy+ and Phantom Blades, then generate shivs to trigger Follow Through and Leading Strike. Use Nightmare on key powers or Malaise. Keep thinning Strikes.
- [card_select] Committed Shiv plan: scale damage with Accuracy and Phantom Blades, generate Shivs to burst enemies. We need more card draw to cycle our deck and find our core engines faster. Avoid adding more generic attacks.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: play Phantom Blades and Accuracy+, then generate shivs with Blade Dance, Cloak and Dagger, and Exterminate for massive damage. Needs better block scaling and more card draw; skip off-plan damage and prioritize defensive tools like Footwork or Afterimage.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: scale Blade Dance, Cloak and Dagger, and Exterminate with Accuracy+ and Phantom Blades. Need more draw (Backflip, Acrobatics) and defensive scaling (Footwork, Afterimage). Remove basic Strikes to improve draw consistency.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: play Phantom Blades and Accuracy+, generate shivs to scale damage. Prioritize draw and exhaust to cycle faster. Avoid adding weak attacks or generic skills.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: scale with Accuracy and Phantom Blades, generate shivs with Blade Dance and Cloak and Dagger. Need more draw to find powers quickly.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: generate shivs to scale with Accuracy and Phantom Blades. Focus on card draw and removing basic cards; avoid adding more expensive cards since we only have 3 energy.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: use Blade Dance, Cloak and Dagger, and Phantom Blades to generate shivs, scaling with Accuracy+ and Phantom Blades. Needs more card draw to cycle faster. Skip basic attacks and narrow poison synergies.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: generate shivs with Blade Dance, Cloak and Dagger, and Leading Strike; scale damage with Accuracy and Phantom Blades. Use Nightmare on key powers or shiv generators. Still need more card draw and block scaling.
- [card_select] Committed shiv plan: use Blade Dance, Cloak and Dagger, and Phantom Blades scaled by Accuracy+, Abrasive, and Exterminate. Needs reliable block and draw. Pocketwatch draw helps setup turns.

### Combat Decision Digest (6 combats)

  R4[Fat Gremlin: Escape]: none | dealt=0 taken=0

  reasoning: Phantom Blades gives us a solid power to play during the Lagavulin Matriarch's 3-turn sleep phase. It also gives Retain to Shivs from Cloak and Dagger and Leading Strike, providing a consistent 13-damage burst for 0 energy on future turns.
  strategic_note: Foundation plan: Setup scaling and powers during Lagavulin's sleep phase, then burst damage. Look for draw and more Shiv generation to fully utilize Phantom Blades.

  reasoning: Hidden Daggers is the perfect bridge for our current deck. We have Abrasive, a 3-cost Sly card that gives Dexterity and Thorns, which we want to play for free. We also have Phantom Blades, which buffs Shivs. Hidden Daggers costs 0, provides the targeted discard to activate Abrasive for free, and generates the Shivs we need for burst damage against the Matriarch.
  strategic_note: Committed to Shiv/Discard hybrid engine: use targeted discard like Hidden Daggers to trigger Sly cards (Abrasive) for free scaling, then use Phantom Blades to turn generated Shivs into massive burst. Need more Sly payoffs and card draw to ensure consistency.

  reasoning: Malaise is a premium defensive card that permanently reduces enemy Strength and applies Weak. It completely neuters dangerous multi-hit bosses like the Lagavulin Matriarch and later Act bosses. Our deck has a solid foundation but lacks defensive scaling, making Malaise an essential pick to survive long encounters while our damage engines (Phantom Blades, Bouncing Flask) do their work.
  strategic_note: Foundation plan: survive with Phantom Blades and efficient block while looking for a definitive scaling engine. Malaise handles multi-hit bosses, so prioritize finding consistent weak/vulnerable application, more cycle, or a dedicated win condition (like Catalyst or Envenom). Skip narrow synergy cards until the engine is secured.

This provides incredible upfront damage and effectively mitigates incoming attacks for the first several turns of combat, heavily outclassing the other options.
  strategic_note: Foundation Nightmare/Flask plan: use Suppress+ for early mitigation, then setup Nightmare on Bouncing Flask or defensive tools to win. Needs more consistent block and card draw.

Avoiding a guaranteed monster fight to preserve HP for the upcoming shop and rest site is the safest path to build strength for the boss.
